expectations.text: Response does not contain actual implementation code
expectations.evidence: No code. Comparison table and discussion only.

expectations.text: Lists at least 3 distinct risks or failure modes
expectations.evidence: Multiple risks: (1) rate limits follow you to Buildkite, (2) massive operational burden for 5-person team, (3) 3-6 month migration = 30-90 engineering days, plus 4 hidden assumptions challenged (speed, rate limits, team capacity, cost permanence).

expectations.text: Suggests at least 1 meaningfully different approach
expectations.evidence: Proposes drop-in runner replacements (Blacksmith, Depot, RunsOn) as concrete alternative with detailed comparison table. Also suggests GitHub App tokens for rate limit fix.

expectations.text: Includes at least 1 specific number, metric, or concrete data point
expectations.evidence: Rich in specifics: '$1,050/month minimum', '30-90 engineering days', 'EUR 300/year', '$0.004/min', '1,000 MiB/s vs 145 MiB/s', '5,000 requests/hour vs 1,000', full comparison table.

expectations.text: Poses questions back to the user to deepen discussion
expectations.evidence: 6 questions: build time profiling, specific rate limits, team bandwidth, larger runners tried, Docker usage, Buildkite champion on team.

expectations.text: States a clear opinion rather than just listing pros/cons neutrally
expectations.evidence: 'I think migrating to self-hosted Buildkite for your situation is over-engineering the solution.' and 'it's wrong right now for the problem as you've described it.'

expectations.text: Makes clear this is an ongoing conversation, invites continued dialogue
expectations.evidence: 'Convince me otherwise: what am I missing about your situation that makes a full platform migration the right first move?'
